_doc: Parity gates for the 3-layer PPTX export (EXPORT-PPTX-3LAYER-PLAN.md §10.2). validate_export_objects.py reads this file — scripts must not hard-code thresholds. max_mean_err is the primary correctness guard (a misplaced/glued/wrong overlay shifts a contiguous region and spikes mean error); max_changed_ratio is a secondary edge-coverage guard. Calibrated 2026-06-12 against the interview-workshop-sunriser-clone deck (12 full-slide vector overlays): faithful registration still yields ~0.1-0.6% 'changed' pixels because the QA-proxy overlay is captured at 2x and LANCZOS-resampled to 1x for compose while the reference is a native 1x render — a sub-pixel edge-antialiasing halo around every vector edge (interiors diff to zero; mean_err stays < 0.34). The delivered PPTX overlay is an embedded vector svgBlip, not this raster proxy, so the proxy resample must not fail a faithful export. max_changed_ratio raised to 0.01 (worst faithful observation 0.0055 + margin); mean_err caps unchanged so real defects still fail.

overlay_coverage._doc: Anti-loophole gate: an overlay whose bounds cover >= max_ratio of the canvas is a renamed background, not a separated object (observed 2026-06-12: full-page visual.svg wrapped in one data-export-layer tag passed the untagged gate while gluing every object into one picture). Legit decor rarely exceeds ~60% of the slide; 0.85 leaves headroom for large hero illustrations while still catching full-bleed artwork. Escape hatch: --allow-full-bleed on validate_export_objects.py.
